title: About the Craftsman
intro: Every cane has a story, and so does the person who makes them.
paragraphs: After suffering a serious back injury while preventing a bariatric patient from harming themselves, I underwent multiple back surgeries. During my recovery, a walking cane became an important part of everyday life. What began as a necessity soon became something much more meaningful.

One day, I purchased an Irish shillelagh. Its rustic craftsmanship, natural wood grain, and timeless character stood apart from the mass-produced canes I had seen before. It wasn't just a mobility aid; it was a beautifully handcrafted piece that carried history, personality, and purpose.
That experience sparked a new passion.

I began learning the craft of woodworking and experimenting with locally sourced hardwoods, shaping each piece by hand and allowing the natural beauty of the wood to guide the finished design. No two trees are the same, and no two canes should be either.

Today, every cane is individually handcrafted with patience, care, and attention to detail. Whether you're looking for a dependable walking companion, a distinctive heirloom, or a custom piece made specifically for you, each cane is created with the same dedication that first inspired me to begin this journey.
My hope is that every cane leaving my workshop offers not only strength and support, but also a piece of craftsmanship that can be appreciated for years to come.
